authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for food safety plans, hazard analysis, preventive controls, monitoring, corrective actions, verification, recall plans, and recordkeeping in human food manufacturing.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for traceability lot codes, shipping and receiving KDEs, 24-hour record retrieval, and outbreak or recall traceability obligations for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability, shipping, receiving, and retrieval handling

authoritative_sources.title: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's guidance is authoritative public workflow guidance for supplier approval, verification activity selection, receiving procedures, and co-manufacturer handling under the preventive-controls rule.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official FAQ set clarifies current implementation posture, compliance timing, sortable-spreadsheet expectations, and practical record-sharing behavior during outbreak or recall handling.

authoritative_sources.title: General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Codex is the globally recognized public baseline for food hygiene and HACCP vocabulary, including corrective action, verification, documentation, training, and lot-marking expectations used across food manufacturing.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's public global traceability standard is a recognized vendor-neutral workflow reference for linking lot, shipment, receipt, and transformation data across supply-chain systems of record.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 117 is also the primary public operating reference for recall plans, corrective action procedures, supplier non-conformance handling, and documented verification before affected food can move.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold, release, and nonconformance boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: food-safety evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review batch, lot, preventive-control, sanitation, allergen, environmental, and release records to determine whether the visible evidence supports a bounded food-safety recommendation.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and nonconformance boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold and release boundaries, identify unresolved evidence gaps, and route final authority to QA, regulatory, engineering, or incident-command owners.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: traceability and inventory conflict analysis
workflow_stages.description: Analyze lot, batch, shipment, receiving, transformation, and inventory records across ERP, WMS, QMS, and traceability repositories to determine whether lineage and hold scope are supportable.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: supplier verification and incident escalation
workflow_stages.description: Assess supplier approval, receiving controls, certificate support, and incident severity to decide when supplier-quality escalation or formal incident routing is required.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, batch records, and lot genealogy logs
food safety plans, hazard analyses, HACCP plans, and preventive-control monitoring records
sanitation records, allergen changeover logs, environmental monitoring results, and deviation investigations
quality holds, release records, nonconformance reports, CAPA packages, and change-control records
supplier approval records, certificates of analysis, receiving inspection evidence, and supplier corrective-action responses
shipping KDEs, receiving KDEs, traceability lot code records, and recall or outbreak retrieval packages

allowed: assess whether visible food-safety evidence supports hold, release recommendation, investigation, corrective-action escalation, or incident status packaging
organize missing records, unresolved lot scope, and required next owners
summarize supplier, traceability, sanitation, allergen, or preventive-control conflicts without overstating authority
escalate: final QA release, recall command, or regulator-facing communication
supplier approval, substitution approval, or use-as-is authority
engineering or process changes that alter validated safety controls
legal, medical, or jurisdiction-specific interpretations that materially change the answer
refuse: override documented hold, release, traceability, or preventive-control boundaries
treat incomplete batch, lot, supplier, or verification records as acceptable proof of release
approve rework, substitution, or distribution where formal quality or supplier approval is still required

domain_failure_modes: traceability records conflict across ERP, WMS, batch, or supplier evidence
product is pressured toward release while hold, deviation, or verification evidence is incomplete
repeat defects or incidents are treated as administratively closed without corrective-action evidence
supplier substitutions or ingredient use decisions are accepted without documented authority
food-safety review drifts into recall command, final release, engineering changes, or legal interpretation

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats jurisdiction as a tenant-supplied prerequisite and refuses to improvise product-category oversight when it would change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability Rule applicability and timing
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack preserves the public baseline and records that FDA's FAQ states FDA does not intend to enforce the rule before July 20, 2028, while still using the rule for covered-workflow terminology and record-shape constraints.

notes: The public baseline supports food-safety evidence handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for exact product scope, jurisdiction, release owners, rework constraints, and supplier approval maps.
The specialist is intentionally structured as operator-side food-safety labor and must not imply final QA release, recall command, legal advice, or supplier-approval authority.
